description: Ongoing Andes hantavirus transmission in rural southern Chile. Cases linked to exposure to long-tailed rice rat (Oligoryzomys longicaudatus) in forested areas.
Andes hantavirus cluster in northern Patagonia. Person-to-person transmission possible with this strain — contact tracing active.
Sin Nombre virus cases reported in the Four Corners region. Exposure linked to deer mouse (Peromyscus maniculatus) in rural dwellings.
Sporadic Sin Nombre virus cases in Washington and Oregon. Risk elevated in rural and camping areas during rodent population peaks.
Choclo virus (HPS) cases reported in the Azuero Peninsula. Reservoir is the pygmy rice rat.
HPS cases in rural São Paulo State. Juquitiba and Araraquara strains circulating. Agricultural workers at elevated risk.
Sporadic Laguna Negra virus cases in lowland Bolivia. Vesper mouse (Calomys laucha) is the primary reservoir.
Isolated Laguna Negra hantavirus case in the Chaco region. No evidence of sustained transmission.
Puumala orthohantavirus outbreak linked to high bank vole (Myodes glareolus) population. Nephropathia epidemica (NE) — milder form, renal syndrome predominant.
Puumala virus season active. Finland reports some of the highest NE incidence in Europe during bank vole peak years.
Puumala hantavirus cases in Norrland. Closely tracks 3-4 year vole population cycle.
Puumala and Hantaan virus cases across Ural and Western Siberia. Russia reports the highest absolute number of HFRS cases in Europe annually.
Hantaan and Seoul virus HFRS cases in Heilongjiang and Jilin provinces. China accounts for over 90% of global HFRS cases annually.
Hantaan virus HFRS cases near the DMZ. The disease was first identified here — historically called Korean hemorrhagic fever.
Sporadic Thailand orthohantavirus cases in Chiang Mai area. Underdiagnosis likely due to symptom overlap with other febrile illnesses.
